cards.title: The Concave Mirror
cards.body: A concave mirror curves inward — like the inside of a bowl. It bends all incoming rays toward a single point in front of the mirror called the Focal Point (F).

cards.title: The Convex Mirror
cards.body: A convex mirror curves outward — like the back of a spoon. It spreads rays outward. Reflected rays never converge, so a convex mirror always forms a smaller, upright virtual image.

cards.title: Object Position Changes Everything
cards.body: Moving the object changes the size, location, and type of image. F (Focal Point) and C (Centre of Curvature) divide the space into regions — each one produces a different kind of image.

cards.title: Real vs Virtual Images
cards.body: A real image forms where reflected rays actually meet in front of the mirror — you can project it on a screen. A virtual image only appears to be behind the mirror — rays don't actually go there.

cards.title: How to Play
cards.body: Drag the white arrow left or right to move the object. The image updates live as you go. Once you think you've met the mission target, press Run Experiment.

missions.title: Real Image — Guided
missions.learningGoal: Discover that a concave mirror forms a real image when the object is placed beyond the focal point.

payload.hint: Drag the object to the LEFT, past F. A real image forms when the object is beyond the focal point.

missions.title: Real Image — Magnified
missions.learningGoal: Understand that placing the object between F and C produces a magnified real image.

payload.hint: Place the object between F and C — the closer to F, the bigger the image.

missions.title: Real Image — Master
missions.learningGoal: Apply the mirror formula to produce exactly 2× magnification.

payload.hint: For 2× magnification, the image must be twice as far from the mirror as the object.

missions.title: Virtual Image — Guided
missions.learningGoal: Discover that virtual images form when the object is inside F or when using a convex mirror.

payload.hint: Try the convex mirror — it always forms upright virtual images. Or move the object inside F with a concave mirror.

missions.title: Virtual Image — Convex
missions.learningGoal: Understand that a convex mirror always forms a diminished virtual image.

payload.hint: Switch to the convex mirror. Move the object further away to make the image smaller.

missions.title: Virtual Image — Master
missions.learningGoal: Produce a precisely diminished virtual image using a convex mirror.

payload.hint: Convex mirror, object far from the mirror — the further away, the smaller the virtual image.
